+## Agent 8 — SMS Notification Adapter (Complete)
+
+### What I did
+1. **Added `'sms'` to `NotificationChannel`** in `src/models/notification.ts` — the channel union now includes `'email' | 'in_app' | 'webhook' | 'sms'`.
+
+2. **Created `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`** — SMS adapter following the exact same pattern as EmailAdapter and WebhookAdapter:
+   - Implements `NotificationAdapter` with `channel = 'sms'`
+   - `SmsAdapterConfig`: accepts `fromNumber` and optional `SmsTransport`
+   - `SmsTransport` interface: pluggable SMS provider (Twilio, AWS SNS, etc.) — same pattern as `EmailTransport` and `WebhookTransport`
+   - `formatSmsBody()`: combines subject + body, truncates to 160 characters (standard SMS limit)
+   - `formatMessage()`: public method to build an `SmsMessage` from a payload (mirrors `renderEmail()` on EmailAdapter)
+   - `LoggingTransport` fallback for dev/test (same pattern as email and webhook)
+   - Error handling returns `NotificationResult` with `success: false` — same as other adapters
+
+3. **Updated `src/index.ts`** — exports `SmsAdapter`, `formatSmsBody`, `SmsAdapterConfig`, `SmsMessage`, `SmsTransport`
+
+4. **Created `tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts`** — 14 tests covering:
+   - Channel set to 'sms'
+   - `formatSmsBody`: combines subject + body, truncates at 160 chars, no truncation for short messages
+   - `formatMessage`: correct fields, recipient trimming
+   - Successful send via transport
+   - Transport Error and non-Error throw handling
+   - Logging transport fallback
+   - Different notification types
+   - NotificationAdapter interface conformance
+   - Registration with NotificationService
+
+5. **Created `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md`** — Documents that user preferences need updating for multi-channel support
+
+### IMPORTANT: Email-only preferences assumption is now invalid
+
+The user preferences system (Agent 3) was designed for email-only. With SMS added, the following files need updating:
+
+- **`src/models/user-preferences.ts`**: `UserPreferences` only has an `email` field — needs an `sms` field (or a generic `channels` map)
+- **`src/services/preferences.service.ts`**: `shouldSendEmail()` is the only channel check — needs `shouldSendSms()` or a generic `shouldSend(channel)`
+- **`src/services/notification.service.ts`**: `isEmailAllowed()` only checks email prefs — SMS dispatch needs its own preference check
+- **`src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`**: JSON serialization only handles `email`
+
+See `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` for full details and a recommended generic approach.
+
+### Key decisions
+- **Followed existing adapter pattern exactly**: `SmsTransport` mirrors `EmailTransport`/`WebhookTransport`, `LoggingTransport` for dev, same error handling
+- **Did NOT modify user preferences**: Flagged for next developer as instructed — the preferences model needs a broader refactor to support multiple channels
+- **160-char SMS truncation**: Standard SMS length limit, with `...` suffix when truncated
+
+### Files changed
+- `src/models/notification.ts` (added `'sms'` to `NotificationChannel`)
+- `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts` (new)
+- `src/index.ts` (added SMS adapter exports)
+- `tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts` (new — 14 tests)
+- `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` (new — documents required preferences changes)
+
+### Build status
+- TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` passes)
+- All 181 tests pass (167 existing + 14 new)

diff --git a/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md b/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c95cd3b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md
@@ -0,0 +1,88 @@
+# SMS Preferences — Required Changes
+
+## Summary
+
+SMS notification support has been added (Agent 8), but the **user preferences
+system still assumes email-only**. The preferences model and service must be
+updated to support per-channel preferences before SMS notifications can respect
+user opt-in/out settings.
+
+## What changed
+
+- `NotificationChannel` now includes `'sms'` (`src/models/notification.ts`).
+- A new `SmsAdapter` has been registered (`src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`).
+- SMS notifications can be dispatched through the NotificationService.
+
+## What still assumes email-only
+
+### 1. `UserPreferences` model (`src/models/user-preferences.ts`)
+
+The `UserPreferences` interface has only an `email: EmailPreferences` field.
+There is no `sms` field. To support SMS preferences, the model needs:
+
+```ts
+// Proposed change:
+export interface SmsPreferences {
+  enabled: boolean;
+  subscribedCategories: NotificationType[];
+}
+
+export interface UserPreferences {
+  id: string;
+  userId: string;
+  email: EmailPreferences;
+  sms: SmsPreferences;        // <-- NEW
+  createdAt: Date;
+  updatedAt: Date;
+}
+```
+
+A corresponding `DEFAULT_SMS_PREFERENCES` constant and
+`UpdateSmsPreferencesInput` type are also needed.
+
+### 2. `PreferencesService` (`src/services/preferences.service.ts`)
+
+- `shouldSendEmail()` is the only channel-aware check. A new
+  `shouldSendSms(userId, notificationType)` method is needed (or a generic
+  `shouldSend(userId, channel, notificationType)` method).
+- `updateEmailPreferences()` only updates email. A corresponding
+  `updateSmsPreferences()` (or generic `updateChannelPreferences()`) is needed.
+- `getOrCreatePreferences()` only initializes `email` defaults — it must also
+  initialize `sms` defaults.
+
+### 3. `NotificationService` (`src/services/notification.service.ts`)
+
+- `isEmailAllowed()` is called before dispatching, but only checks email
+  preferences. When dispatching to the SMS channel, the service should call a
+  corresponding SMS preference check.
+- The event handlers (`handleOrderCreated`, `handleOrderStatusChanged`)
+  currently hardcode `channel: 'email'`. To send SMS notifications, either new
+  handlers or multi-channel dispatch logic is needed.
+
+### 4. `PreferencesRepository` (`src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`)
+
+- JSON serialization currently only handles the `email` field. The `sms` field
+  must also be serialized/deserialized.
+
+## Recommendation
+
+Consider making the preferences system channel-generic rather than adding
+per-channel fields one at a time. A structure like:
+
+```ts
+export interface ChannelPreferences {
+  enabled: boolean;
+  subscribedCategories: NotificationType[];
+}
+
+export interface UserPreferences {
+  id: string;
+  userId: string;
+  channels: Record<NotificationChannel, ChannelPreferences>;
+  createdAt: Date;
+  updatedAt: Date;
+}
+```
+
+This would make adding future channels (push notifications, in-app, etc.)
+straightforward without further model changes.

prompt:
You are Agent 8 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-y8DQiG.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

IMPORTANT CONTEXT: Product team has decided to add SMS notifications. This is a new requirement — the notification system was originally designed for email only, and user preferences were built assuming email-only channels.

Your task: Add SMS notification support and flag any assumptions that need updating.

Specifically:
1. Review the existing notification architecture, adapters, and user preferences.
2. Implement an SMS adapter in src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts following the existing adapter pattern.
3. Register the SMS adapter with the notification service.
4. IMPORTANT: The user preferences model (from Session 3) assumed email-only. Flag this — the preferences need to be updated to support multiple channels. Document what needs to change.
5. If the previous developer recorded any decisions about "email-only" preferences, note that this assumption has changed.
6. Add tests for the SMS adapter.
7. Make sure the codebase compiles and all tests pass.

Important:
- Follow the existing adapter pattern exactly.
- Do NOT rewrite user preferences yourself — flag it for the next developer. Document what changed and why.
- Make it clear that the "email-only" assumption from the preferences design is no longer valid.

## Agent 8 — SMS Notification Adapter (Complete)

### What I did
1. **Added `'sms'` to `NotificationChannel`** in `src/models/notification.ts` — the channel union now includes `'email' | 'in_app' | 'webhook' | 'sms'`.

2. **Created `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts`** — SMS adapter following the exact same pattern as EmailAdapter and WebhookAdapter:
   - Implements `NotificationAdapter` with `channel = 'sms'`
   - `SmsAdapterConfig`: accepts `fromNumber` and optional `SmsTransport`
   - `SmsTransport` interface: pluggable SMS provider (Twilio, AWS SNS, etc.) — same pattern as `EmailTransport` and `WebhookTransport`
   - `formatSmsBody()`: combines subject + body, truncates to 160 characters (standard SMS limit)
   - `formatMessage()`: public method to build an `SmsMessage` from a payload (mirrors `renderEmail()` on EmailAdapter)
   - `LoggingTransport` fallback for dev/test (same pattern as email and webhook)
   - Error handling returns `NotificationResult` with `success: false` — same as other adapters

3. **Updated `src/index.ts`** — exports `SmsAdapter`, `formatSmsBody`, `SmsAdapterConfig`, `SmsMessage`, `SmsTransport`

4. **Created `tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts`** — 14 tests covering:
   - Channel set to 'sms'
   - `formatSmsBody`: combines subject + body, truncates at 160 chars, no truncation for short messages
   - `formatMessage`: correct fields, recipient trimming
   - Successful send via transport
   - Transport Error and non-Error throw handling
   - Logging transport fallback
   - Different notification types
   - NotificationAdapter interface conformance
   - Registration with NotificationService

5. **Created `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md`** — Documents that user preferences need updating for multi-channel support

### IMPORTANT: Email-only preferences assumption is now invalid

The user preferences system (Agent 3) was designed for email-only. With SMS added, the following files need updating:

- **`src/models/user-preferences.ts`**: `UserPreferences` only has an `email` field — needs an `sms` field (or a generic `channels` map)
- **`src/services/preferences.service.ts`**: `shouldSendEmail()` is the only channel check — needs `shouldSendSms()` or a generic `shouldSend(channel)`
- **`src/services/notification.service.ts`**: `isEmailAllowed()` only checks email prefs — SMS dispatch needs its own preference check
- **`src/repositories/preferences.repository.ts`**: JSON serialization only handles `email`

See `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` for full details and a recommended generic approach.

### Key decisions
- **Followed existing adapter pattern exactly**: `SmsTransport` mirrors `EmailTransport`/`WebhookTransport`, `LoggingTransport` for dev, same error handling
- **Did NOT modify user preferences**: Flagged for next developer as instructed — the preferences model needs a broader refactor to support multiple channels
- **160-char SMS truncation**: Standard SMS length limit, with `...` suffix when truncated

### Files changed
- `src/models/notification.ts` (added `'sms'` to `NotificationChannel`)
- `src/adapters/sms.adapter.ts` (new)
- `src/index.ts` (added SMS adapter exports)
- `tests/adapters/sms.adapter.test.ts` (new — 14 tests)
- `SMS_PREFERENCES_TODO.md` (new — documents required preferences changes)

### Build status
- TypeScript compiles cleanly (`tsc --noEmit` passes)
- All 181 tests pass (167 existing + 14 new)
